note: docs type — free-skip
diff:
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/decisions/dev-guardrails-distribution.md
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+# Target: dev-guardrails-distribution
+
+## Why
+Guardrail configs (biome, eslint, hooks) drift across packages when
+copied by hand; this Target tracks moving them into a single
+distributable package consumers extend from.

+## Status: proposed

message: fix(hooks): protected-branch-guard misses rtk/space-prefixed commits + crashed on bash 3.2
nameStatus:
M	.claude/hooks/protected-branch-guard.sh
A	.claude/hooks/protected-branch-guard.test.mjs
M	scripts/git/ship-branch.sh
A	scripts/git/ship-branch.test.mjs
note: Fixes a regex anchor and a bash-3.2 heredoc crash in a repo git-hook (.claude/hooks) plus a test seam in scripts/git — developer tooling, not the shipped Electron/socket/serverless runtime Sentry instruments, despite the error-flavored subject.
diff:
--- a/.claude/hooks/protected-branch-guard.sh
+++ b/.claude/hooks/protected-branch-guard.sh
@@ -22,9 +22,9 @@ is_protected_branch() {
-  if [[ "$branch" =~ ^main$|^release/ ]]; then
+  if [[ "$branch" =~ ^(rtk[[:space:]]+)?(main|release/.*)$ ]]; then
     return 0
   fi
   return 1
 }
@@ -40,7 +40,7 @@ block_commit() {
-  cat <<MSG
+  cat <<'MSG'
   Commits to protected branches are blocked.
 MSG
 }

message: fix(ship-branch): drop the branch on a commit failure (empty), keep it on a push failure (has work)
note: Error-handling wording, but the change is entirely in scripts/git/ship-branch.sh cleanup trap — repo dev tooling, not the shipped Electron/socket/serverless runtime Sentry instruments.
diff:
--- a/scripts/git/ship-branch.sh
+++ b/scripts/git/ship-branch.sh
@@ -22,10 +22,14 @@ cleanup_on_failure() {
-  git branch -D "$branch_name" 2>/dev/null || true
+  if [ "$commit_created" = "false" ]; then
+    git branch -D "$branch_name" 2>/dev/null || true
+  else
+    echo "keeping branch $branch_name (has commits, push failed)"
+  fi
 }
 
 trap cleanup_on_failure ERR
 
-git commit -m "$msg"
+git commit -m "$msg" && commit_created=true
 git push origin "$branch_name"
